summary: Story, Architecture, canonical Spec, generated Task projection, code invariants, and focused regression coverage consistently define the one-command closure and the schema 0.2.0 selected-route reconciliation at HEAD a9109350.
inspection_summary: Compared the Story acceptance criteria and explicit delivery-reconciliation task with the Architecture ownership boundary, Spec invariant INV-OCR-7, generated Task projection, origin/main diff, current execution-state route selection, and focused regression/E2E coverage. The implementation-commit concern from the prior planning review is resolved on this surface: implement now requires HEAD advancement and has a fail-closed regression test.
inspection_evidence: git diff --check origin/main...HEAD; node --test test/one-command-pr-ready-closure.test.js test/execution-state.test.js test/e2e/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure-runtime.spec.ts (72/72 pass)
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the previous spec-consistency finding reported that implement could accept an unchanged managed-worktree HEAD -> Final conclusion: src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js passes requireHeadAdvance=true for implement, and OCR-T-3 asserts the same-HEAD path returns typed no_progress.

Initial concern: schema 0.2.0 reconciliation could still derive ownership by resolving an undeclared probe Story -> Final conclusion: managedWorktreeArtifactLocations resolves only the selected Story route, determines directory scope from its canonical template, and unit plus Story-E2E fixtures prove managed/source synchronization for a named feature_slug profile.

Initial concern: the added reconciliation task might be only documented rather than reflected in the generated Task surface -> Final conclusion: the generated delivery-reconciliation task lists all seven Story target paths as done and its acceptance criteria match Story OCR-S-9, Architecture, Spec, implementation, and tests.
